mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and stock prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If a rising INDPRO (positive monthly change) is followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE > 5.0 but SPX 60-day returns are positive in at least 60% of instances over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and employment gains signal a robust expansion in demand and labor market, driving up equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2% and PAYEMS monthly growth > 0%
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but SPX 20-day return is negative in >40% of cases over a 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate environment (below 1%), an increase in FEDFUNDS signals policy normalization and confidence in the economy, which is positive for equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1%, the forward 20-day SPX return is negative more often than positive following an upward move in FEDFUNDS, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which immediately pressures corporate revenues and stock prices over the following week.
failure_condition: If a 1%+ drop in RSAFS does not lead to negative SPX 5-day returns with a hit rate above 60%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate increased residential construction activity, which boosts overall economic growth and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If an increase in HOUST (positive monthly change) is followed by negative SPX 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises and unemployment falls simultaneously, the economy is operating at full capacity with strong labor absorption, boosting corporate earnings and stock prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combination of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE yields negative SPX 20-day returns more than 30% of the time over a 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI growth below 2% year-over-year), an increase in CPIAUCSL signals strengthening aggregate demand without overheating, which is positive for equities over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If during low CPI growth periods, rising CPI leads to negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns over a 5-year window, reject.
